items.title: Doubt hangs over much-awaited Queanbeyan respite centre

children.content: The federal government committed $1 million to support the initial operations of Yvonne Cuschieri House, but that has not been enough to secure a service provider — and without more funding the future of the centre is uncertain.

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Regional Australia leads GP bulk-billing surge

children.content: A new report shows regional and remote towns are rapidly lifting their GP bulk-billing rates while out-of-pocket costs rise across the country.

image.altText: generic photo of psychologist's hand holding a pen and notepad with a patient

items.title: High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

gallery.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

thumbnail.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

items.title: 53 witnesses, 97 exhibits: Murder trial jury faces 'complex' task

children.content: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

image.altText: A woman smiles with the sun on her face.

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant factor' in Canberra teen's drowning death

children.content: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

image.altText: A pond with two road bridges going over it.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith display at War Memorial altered following arrest

children.content: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey hair wearing a navy suit stands outdoors looking serious.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey hair wearing a navy suit stands outdoors looking serious.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: 'They were already out of their seats': The moment Artemis II astronauts knew they were home safe

children.content: Nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds, a journey deeper into space than ever before and a rapt Earth watching on. This is how four astronauts made history.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

items.title: Why did we go back to the Moon, 50 years after Apollo?

children.content: In both 1968 and 2026, a mission to the Moon offered a refreshing blast of positivity for a conflict-ridden world. So what's different now — and what do we really gain from this new moonshot, five decades after the first?

image.altText: Two side by side images of a moon landscape on the bottom and a partial Earth circle in the top right corners as seen in space.

items.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it

children.content: It was "the best Canberra scoop in years", but the journalist's own editors did not believe it, and almost buried the story.

image.altText: Old Parliament House, Canberra.

items.title: I thought I understood China. Then I ordered a craft beer

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

thumbnail.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

items.title: Queensland e-bike users say commute time could 'roughly double' under new laws

children.content: E-bike advocates say new speed limits, set to come into effect from July, go against current guidelines and could create unsafe situations.

items.title: Nick Xenophon-founded SA Best party comes to an end

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

items.title: The nannasphere is an antidote to the world's grief and horrors

children.content: Surely one of the best ways to counter the hate, grief and horrors of the world is to foster communities driven by decency, calm and care. Enter: the nannasphere.

items.title: 'It's taken me all over the world': Life as a second-generation human cannonball

items.title: Opposition plans to recruit overseas officers to fill Victoria Police vacancies
